whatWasImplemented: Wrote .env.example documenting every env var in services.yaml's env_requirements — day-1 (Supabase, OpenAI, Firecrawl, app URL), M2 (Stripe trio), M8 (Upstash, Resend, Sentry), plus test-only (SUPABASE_TEST_*). Each section commented with which milestone brings it online.

Added scripts/secret-scan.sh — scans staged git diff for sk_live_*, rk_live_*, sk_test_{20+}, whsec_*, sk-openai keys, AWS AKIA keys, Google AIza keys, and PEM private-key blocks. Exits 1 with a clear message when any match is found. macOS-BSD-grep compatible (uses `-oE --`, no empty alternations).

Added .githooks/pre-commit that runs the scanner. Repo is configured via the `prepare` npm script to set `git config core.hooksPath .githooks` on `pnpm install`, so the hook activates on every developer machine without husky.
Added README.md 'Secrets' section naming Vercel as the production secret store and documenting the pre-commit scanner.

Added src/__tests__/env-audit.test.ts with 25 assertions: .env.example exists and documents all 13 expected vars, .env.local has never been committed + is gitignored, pre-commit hook + scanner are present and executable, the scanner detects sk_test_ keys and passes clean diffs, package.json prepare wires hooksPath, and every env_requirements entry in services.yaml round-trips to .env.example.
discoveredIssues: No pre-existing husky/lint-staged setup — chose raw .githooks + core.hooksPath so the install footprint stays minimal (no new runtime deps).
Scanner treats .env.example, package-lock.json, and pnpm-lock.yaml as excluded — changes to those files are not scanned (real secrets would not live there).

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run — 223 passed / 1 skipped / 31 files.

manualChecks.action: git config core.hooksPath after pnpm install's prepare script
scanner vs staged diff containing a Stripe-live-style placeholder
scanner vs staged diff containing a PEM private-key header
